title: GitHub Actions の 409 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article accurately identifies common causes and solutions for HTTP 409 errors in GitHub Actions, largely supported by official documentation and community discussions.
Most factual claims are well-grounded with current and relevant sources.

The freshness score reflects that while many sources are recent, a specific GitHub Enterprise version (2.20) is mentioned, which might be dated for GitHub Enterprise Cloud, though the underlying policy principles remain valid.

Two minor claims about exact error message phrasing and the explicit purpose of a specific deployment ID could not be definitively sourced as official definitions, but their underlying concepts are accurate.

sources.title: 409 Conflict - HTTP status code explained
sources.claim: A 409 Conflict means the request is well-formed but conflicts with the current resource state (concurrent update, duplicate creation).

sources.title: How to Fix 409 Conflict Error: A Complete Guide to Resolving HTTP 409 Code
sources.claim: The 409 Conflict error is defined in RFC 9110, the HTTP Semantics standard maintained by the IETF. According to that specification, a 409 response indicates that the request could not be completed due to a conflict with the current state of the target resource.

sources.title: HTTP/1.1: Status Code Definitions
sources.claim: The request could not be completed due to a conflict with the current state of the resource.

sources.title: List of HTTP status codes - Wikipedia
sources.claim: Indicates that the request could not be processed because of conflict in the current state of the resource, such as an edit conflict between multiple simultaneous updates.

sources.title: 409 Conflict - HTTP - MDN Web Docs - Mozilla
sources.claim: The HTTP 409 Conflict client error response status code indicates a request conflict with the current state of the target resource.

sources.title: 409 Conflict | HTTP Cats
sources.claim: The HTTP 409 Conflict response status code indicates a request conflict with the current state of the target resource.

sources.title: Deploy to Azure App Service fails with "Encountered a retriable status code: 409. Message: 'Conflict'" · Issue #7 - GitHub
sources.claim: Using github actions with azure/webapps-deploy@v1 fails with a 409 response.

sources.title: How to fix Azure DevOps app service deployment always responding with 409 Conflict on first time trying to deploy - Microsoft Learn
sources.claim: Conflict (CODE: 409)" is a common error that occurs when deploying an Azure web app.

sources.title: Azure Failed to Deploy Web App Service CODE: 409 - Microsoft Learn
sources.claim: The 409 Conflict means the deployment endpoint (Kudu/SCM) is in a conflicting state. This is a common issue and is often caused by: Another deployment already in progress or overlapping pipeline runs.

sources.title: How to fix "Deployment endpoint responded with status code 409" error when no deployment is on going - Microsoft Learn
sources.claim: WARNING: Deployment endpoint responded with status code 409. ERROR: There may be an ongoing deployment or your app setting has WEBSITE_RUN_FROM_PACKAGE.

sources.title: GitHub API: How to Handle 409 Conflict Errors When Modifying Repository Files
sources.claim: The 409 Conflict error usually happens due to branch protection or version mismatch. Here's a quick fix: Ensure File Exists: Confirm the file exists at /{file_path}. Use SHA for Updating: Retrieve the file's current SHA before updating.

sources.title: GitHub API: 409 Conflict When Adding or Updating Repository Content - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: I resolved it by getting the latest sha for the specific file and added the sha in update api body.

sources.title: Error 409 Conflict with "Create or Update File Contents" REST API after 5-10 Successful Attempts · community · Discussion #62198 - GitHub
sources.claim: After successfully using the "Create or Update File Contents" REST API to update files in our repository for 5-10 times, we encounter an error with HTTP status code 409 Conflict.

sources.title: 409 Error when updating a repo's file · Issue #1815 - GitHub
sources.claim: 409 is Conflict, I'm guessing GitHub is hashing the file in question (hence ), and get back something different from it expects. My theory here is GitHub hasn't updated the storage from your last update before you send another one, leading to this explosion.

sources.title: REST API endpoints for GitHub Actions permissions
sources.claim: You can use the REST API to set permissions for the organizations and repositories that are allowed to run GitHub Actions, and the actions and reusable workflows that are allowed to run.

sources.title: REST API endpoints for GitHub Actions permissions - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
sources.claim: You can use the REST API to set permissions for the enterprises, organizations and repositories that are allowed to run GitHub Actions, and the actions and reusable workflows that are allowed to run.

sources.title: REST API endpoints for GitHub Actions Secrets
sources.claim: 409. Conflict when visibility type is not set to selected.

sources.title: REST API endpoints for GitHub Actions variables
sources.claim: 409. Response when the visibility of the variable is not set to selected.

sources.title: REST API endpoints for pull requests - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: 409. Conflict if sha was provided and pull request head did not match.

sources.title: Always getting a 409 when I try to merge via Github API - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: I've started getting 409 responses from the github API for every merge call, which corresponds to a merge conflict.

sources.title: Using the REST API to interact with your Git database - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
sources.claim: The REST API will return a 409 Conflict if the Git repository is empty or unavailable.

sources.title: Troubleshooting HTTP 409's when deploying to Azure App Service -
sources.claim: If multiple deployment methods were attempted, and all return a 409 - then this is likely a sign of a deployment that is remaining in a “pending state”. This may cause locks to still exit to prevent concurrent access to files. Resolution... Try deleting the temp-* folder and pending. Afterwards, restart the site.

sources.title: Getting a lot of 409 Conflict errors in response and the error message is unclear · Issue #1787 - GitHub
sources.claim: Fix the issue by git tree approach to update bulk files at once.

sources.title: REST API endpoints for workflows - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: List repository workflows. Lists the workflows in a repository.

sources.title: Enabling debug logging - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: If the workflow logs do not provide enough detail to diagnose why a workflow, job, or step is not working as expected, you can enable additional debug logging. ... To enable step debug logging, set the following secret or variable in the repository that contains the workflow: ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG to true.

sources.title: GitHub Actions — Enable debug logging | by Amit Kumar Dube (अमित दुबे) | Medium
sources.claim: The debug logging in GitHub Actions workflow can be enabled at 2 levels. Runner debug logging — Provides more information about runner and worker. Step debug logging — Provides more information about workflow steps.

sources.title: How to Debug GitHub Actions Workflow Failures - OneUptime
sources.claim: GitHub Actions provides two levels of debug logging: Step Debug Logging. Enable verbose output for all steps: Go to Settings > Secrets and variables > Actions; Add repository variable (or secret): ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG = true.

sources.title: Quick Tip: Hook Into GitHub Actions' Debug Mode - hisaac.net
sources.claim: The way this toggle works is that it sets a secret named ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG to true. GitHub's step execution process then reads this value, and prints out the extra information.

sources.title: GitHub Actions: Re-run jobs with debug logging
sources.claim: You can now enable debug logging when you re-run jobs in a GitHub Actions workflow run. This gives you additional information about the job's execution and its environment.

sources.title: Debug your GitHub Actions Workflows #github #actions #githubactions #programming #devops - YouTube
sources.claim: one of the ways you can turn on debugging is with a variable or a secret. so you create a variable or a secret. called actions_step_debug and you set it equal to true.

sources.title: Workflow commands for GitHub Actions
sources.claim: Prints a debug message to the log. You must create a secret named ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG with the value true to see the debug messages set by this command in the log.

sources.title: Diagnostic Log Stream · projectkudu/kudu Wiki - GitHub
sources.claim: The logstream endpoint (/logstream in the Kudu service) enables developers to view real time traces as they are being written. Using /logstream. The simplest way to get streaming logs is to use curl , e.g.. curl -u {username} https://{sitename}.scm.azurewebsites.net/api/logstream.

sources.title: Display Azure App logs live stream on chromecast (or any webpages) - Stuff & Tacos
sources.claim: FINALLY found this page on kudu Diagnostic Log Stream which make me cry (above work took some time & frustration), with a simple url xxxx.scm.azurewebsites.net/api/logstream and necessary auth header, you can directly get the stream with curl.

sources.title: A Better Way to View Logs in Kudu for Azure App Service on Linux
sources.claim: To make this easier, we have added a new Log stream page in Kudu for Azure App Service on Linux, available under the Logs dropdown. This experience gives you a single place to stream, browse, search, and filter logs so you can understand what is happening in your app faster.

sources.title: Diagnostic Log Stream · projectkudu/kudu Wiki - GitHub
sources.claim: Logstream endpoint (/logstream) enables developers to view real time traces as they are being written.

sources.title: az webapp log | Microsoft Learn
sources.claim: az webapp log tail. Start live log tracing for a web app.

sources.title: az webapp - Microsoft Learn
sources.claim: az webapp log tail. Start live log tracing for a web app.

sources.title: azure CLI 2.0 az webapp log tail cannot find the 'NAME' of my webapp - Server Fault
sources.claim: az webapp log tail -g <rg name> -n <webapp name>.

sources.title: Retrieving Azure Web Apps diagnostics logs - Jorge Bernhardt
sources.claim: To establish streaming of logs to your local terminal, run the following command, and you are now prepared to invoke your API and generate log entries. az webapp log tail --resource-group RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME --name WEB_APP_NAME.

sources.title: Web API logging with Azure CLI (.NET 7) | by Armandas Rokas - Medium
sources.claim: To establish streaming of logs to your local terminal, run the following command, and you are now prepared to invoke your API and generate log entries. az webapp log tail --resource-group RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME --name WEB_APP_NAME.

unsupported_claims: "There is a deployment currently in progress. Please try again when it completes." → エラー本体：別のデプロイがまだ実行中であることを明示 (Error body: explicitly states that another deployment is still in progress.)
scm-deployment-id → デプロイメント ID：進行中のデプロイを特定するための識別子 (Deployment ID: an identifier for identifying an ongoing deployment.)
improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.
Optional improvement: Verify the exact phrasing of example error messages against official documentation if available, or clarify they are illustrative examples.
Optional improvement: Clarify the relevance of GitHub Enterprise 2.20 in the context of potentially newer GitHub Enterprise Cloud versions, or if it refers to a specific on-premise version.
Optional improvement: Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
Optional improvement: Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.
